small_talk_bridge.topic_hook: AI agents becoming real consultants in 2026
small_talk_bridge.bridge: You know how everyone said AI would replace consultants? Turns out it's making them better instead. The smart firms are turning their people into half-engineer, half-strategist hybrids.
small_talk_bridge.when_to_use: At Atlantis brunch or DIFC networking events when tech transformation comes up

arabic.cultural_story: The unofficial currency of the Gulf. Not corruption—it's about leveraging relationships and reputation to open doors. An Emirati might say 'I have wasta with the municipality' meaning they can expedite permits through legitimate but relationship-based channels. Essential for business here.
arabic.practice_sentence: Building wasta with the right people at ADGM has been crucial for our enterprise AI partnerships.

habitus.title: DIFC Art Night Circuit
habitus.content: Every first Thursday, Gallery Walk at DIFC turns into Dubai's most concentrated networking event for finance and tech leaders. Start at Cuadro Fine Art (6:30pm), move to Opera Gallery (7:30pm), finish at Segreto rooftop (8:30pm). The real conversations happen between galleries, not inside them. AED 0 entry, but expect AED 300-500 for drinks.
habitus.why_it_matters: Key LPs, family office principals, and regional tech executives all show up—better deal flow intelligence than any formal conference.

five_sentences: Instead of traditional pre-sales engineers who demo and disappear, these are technical consultants who stay through implementation and get compensated based on customer success metrics.
Started at Palantir and now spreading to Snowflake, Databricks, and enterprise AI platforms as software becomes more complex to deploy.
Snowflake pays their Forward Deployed Engineers up to 40% of account expansion—they're building customer solutions, not just selling them.
This is exactly the FDE evolution Patrik is driving: turning pre-sales from cost center to profit center by making engineers accountable for customer outcomes.
Will this model work for smaller AI platforms, or does it only scale with enterprise price points above $1M ARR?
